discovery.run_summary: Slate is dominated by SaaS names where the earnings 8-K exhibits all fell back to cover pages — no actual prepared financials or guidance text to read, materially degrading the filings signal. The most readable filings setups are MDB (CEO departure + 10-K disclosure of slowing Atlas growth from existing applications) and VSCO (10-K quantifies ~$85M tariff hit and notes ongoing supply-chain disruption). GTLB pre-announced an in-line Q1 and a RIF, which converts that name to a RATIONAL skip.

discoveries.name: MongoDB
discoveries.filings_evidence: Q3 10-Q (filed 2025-12-02) explicitly states 'adverse macroeconomic conditions resulted in slower than historical growth of our existing Atlas applications for the nine months ended October 31, 2025' — a rare specific concession on the consumption engine that drives 73% of revenue per the FY26 10-K. Compounding the risk, the Q4 8-K (2026-03-02) disclosed CEO Ittycheria's resignation effective Nov 9 and President of Field Operations Cedric Pech's resignation effective Apr 15, 2026, plus a new CAO from Confluent — significant go-to-market leadership turnover heading into the Q1 print.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: Cover-page-only 8-Ks prevent direct read of guide vs actual, but the 10-Q language about Atlas existing-application deceleration is the kind of disclosure typically associated with conservative near-term forward commentary.
discoveries.setup: Q1 FY27 print 4 trading days out, first quarter under new CEO CJ Desai with field leadership simultaneously turning over, against an Atlas consumption backdrop the company itself flagged as slowing.
discoveries.thesis: Filings show Atlas consumption deceleration in the existing base (the engine of the model), and the company is simultaneously absorbing a CEO transition and the announced departure of the head of Field Operations — a combination that typically produces conservative first-quarter-under-new-CEO guidance and elevated execution risk on enterprise expansion. Market sentiment around MDB has historically swung on Atlas commentary; a soft or 'reset' guide is plausible and not fully priced.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sequential Atlas growth deceleration, lowered FY27 outlook framed around 'macro' or 'optimization,' commentary acknowledging field-sales disruption from Pech departure, or net-new $100K+ customer adds decelerating from prior quarter.
discoveries.what_kills: Atlas re-acceleration commentary, raised FY guide, evidence Voyage AI / AI workload monetization is offsetting core deceleration, or net expansion rate stable/up versus prior quarter.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a company concedes deceleration in its dominant consumption product in a 10-Q AND simultaneously turns over both CEO and head of field sales, the next print is more often a reset than a beat — track this pattern across consumption-SaaS names.
research_pointers: Pull the Q4 FY26 (Mar 2 2026) press release for actual FY27 guide and Atlas % of revenue trend
Check whether new CEO Desai has signaled a strategic review or guidance reset framework
Look at $100K+ and $1M+ customer count trajectory across last 4 Qs
Track Atlas net new ARR vs consensus expectations going in

discoveries.name: Victoria's Secret & Co.
discoveries.filings_evidence: FY25 10-K (filed 2026-03-20) quantifies tariffs net of mitigation as an ~$85M FY25 operating-income headwind and explicitly flags ongoing supply-chain disruption and uncertainty about future tariff levels post the Feb 2026 Supreme Court IEEPA ruling. The 10-K also discloses a new CFO/COO (Sekella, formerly of JOANN which went into Chapter 11) elevated August 2025 and a new CMO May 2025 — multiple key-officer changes through fiscal 2025. Q3 10-Q (Dec 2025) carries no material change to risk factors.
discoveries.guidance_pattern: 8-K exhibits not isolable, but the cadence of issuing both quarterly and 'updated' full-year guidance every quarter through 2025 indicates management has been actively recalibrating — consistent with a company managing through a moving tariff backdrop.
discoveries.setup: Q1 FY26 print 7 trading days out, first full quarter of guidance under newly seated CFO/COO and CMO, with the 10-K already framing tariffs as a material 2025 P&L hit and the post-IEEPA-ruling 10% Section 122 global tariff regime still settling.
discoveries.thesis: The filings explicitly tell investors that tariffs cost ~$85M in FY25 net of mitigation and that the path forward depends on factors outside management's control (Section 122 tariffs, retaliation, mitigation lead times). Mall-anchored apparel retailers with new finance leadership and an explicit tariff disclosure tend to guide cautiously on the first print of the new fiscal year. The market may be under-weighting how persistent the gross-margin pressure could be into Q1/Q2.
discoveries.what_confirms: Q1 gross margin pressure attributed to tariffs/sourcing costs, FY26 EPS guide trimmed, mention of incremental mitigation costs or supplier shifts, soft North American store comps.
discoveries.what_kills: Tariff refunds from IEEPA reversal materially recognized, gross margin expansion, raised FY guide, strong PINK or Beauty momentum offsetting the tariff drag.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a retailer quantifies a specific tariff dollar impact in the prior-year 10-K AND has new finance leadership, the first guide of the new fiscal year tends to bake in conservative tariff math — read the 10-K dollar figure as a floor, not a ceiling.
research_pointers: Pull the March 5 2026 Q4 press release for the initial FY26 guide and tariff commentary
Check whether Feb 2026 IEEPA Supreme Court ruling results in refund accruals
Monitor mall-traffic and apparel-peer commentary for May
Inventory levels vs Q1 2025 — buildup could signal forced promotional activity

skipped.reason: All 4 earnings 8-Ks fell back to cover-page primary documents — no prepared financials or guidance text readable; cannot form a directional view from filings alone. UNCLEAR.

Filings show clear pressure (DBNR 97% Core / 96% large, COO and GC resignations Dec 2025, ongoing losses) but 8-K exhibits unreadable and Q3 already disclosed updated FY26 guidance — mix of bearish structural signals already in the tape with no fresh quantitative edge from the cover-page-only releases. RATIONAL.
Company pre-announced May 11 2026 that Q1 FY27 will be in-line with prior guidance AND reaffirmed FY27 guide; that 8-K removes the directional surprise window and converts the print to RATIONAL.
